title: Kakurasu
intro: Kakurasu is a logic puzzle where some cells must be marked black () to meet specific requirements.
weights_explanation: Columns are numbered 1, 2, 3, … at the top; rows are numbered 1, 2, 3, … on the left. These numbers serve as weights.
board_example: Below is an empty example board:
rule_row_sum: To the right of each row is a target sum: when you add the weights (column numbers) of the black cells in that row, the result must equal the target.
rule_col_sum: Beneath each column is a target sum: when you add the weights (row numbers) of the black cells in that column, the result must equal the target.
goal: Your goal is to mark some cells black, so that all row and column sums are matched.
multiple_solutions_note: Some targets can be achieved in multiple ways but only one arrangement satisfies all row and column sums together.
example_row: For example, row 2 with target 5 could be 1+4 or 2+3, as in the example shown below.
example_col: Similarly, column 1 in the board with target 6 below, could be 3 + 2 + 1 or 4 + 2
solved: The puzzle is solved when all the row and column sums are satisfied together, as in the example board shown below.
control_cycle: Left click to cycle forward, right click to cycle backward between:

compact.intro: Mark cells black () so that
compact.rule_row: Each row's black cells, multiplied by their column values (on top), equals the number to the right of that row.
compact.rule_col: Each column's black cells, multiplied by their row values (on the left), equals the number at the bottom of that column.
